subtitle: Very common in marathon runners after mile 15

strava_risk_triggers.risk_note: Core fatigue after 2+ hours of running is the primary trigger

mild.description: Stiffness or ache in the lower back after long runs
mild.can_run_note: Yes — focus on posture cues during runs
why: Weak glutes forcing the lumbar spine to compensate for propulsion
Anterior pelvic tilt from tight hip flexors due to sitting
Core fatigue causing spinal compression on long efforts
relief: Child's pose hold for 60s × 3 immediately after runs
Knees-to-chest stretch lying on your back
Heat pad on lower back for 20 min
prevention: Glute bridges and deadbugs — 3× per week minimum
Hip flexor couch stretch daily — 90s each side
Shorten stride to reduce lower back loading
Maintain upright posture — tall spine, slight forward lean from ankles

moderate.description: Pain during runs that worsens with distance, stiff next morning
moderate.can_run_note: Shorter runs only — stop if pain radiates to glutes or legs
why: Lumbar muscle fatigue and spasm from sustained poor posture
Disc irritation from repetitive compressive loading
Weak deep core failing to stabilise the spine under load
relief: Reduce long run distance by 50% for 2 weeks
Daily core activation: plank, deadbug, bird-dog
Piriformis and hip flexor stretching twice daily
prevention: Build core endurance before building mileage
Address sitting posture — lumbar support at desk
Introduce running cadence drills to reduce impact
Swim or cycle on non-running days to maintain mobility

severe.description: Pain radiating into glutes or legs, pain at rest
severe.can_run_note: Do not run — possible nerve involvement
why: Possible disc bulge or nerve root irritation
Significant lumbar muscle spasm limiting movement
Sciatica-like referral pattern needing proper diagnosis
relief: Complete rest from running and heavy lifting
See a physiotherapist or GP urgently if pain radiates below the knee
Gentle walking is fine — avoid sitting for long periods
Heat alternating with ice for muscle spasm
prevention: Do not return to running until pain-free with walking for 1 week
Structured core rehabilitation programme under physio guidance
Imaging if neurological symptoms present (numbness, tingling)

see_physio_if: Pain radiates down one or both legs
Numbness or tingling in legs or feet
No improvement after 1 week of rest
